name: Hyderabad State (1900–1948)
description: A vertical bicolour of green at the hoist and red at the fly, with a white crescent and five-pointed star in the centre.
history: Flag of the princely state of Hyderabad (1900–1947), the largest and wealthiest of the Indian princely states under British paramountcy, ruled by the Asaf Jahi Nizams. The Nizam's own dynastic standard (the Asafia flag) was a separate yellow flag. After Indian independence, Hyderabad was annexed by India in Operation Polo (September 1948).
